info.title: Axene Mailer API (public)
info.description: Email sending platform by Axene Solutions

get.description:
List all domains the authenticated user can see (their own + every
org-mate's domains in the active workspace).

schema.title: Response List Domains Endpoint V1 Domains  Get

post.description: Create a new domain with DKIM keys and DNS records.

get.description:
Eager check used by the add-domain UI: looks up axene-verify TXTs in
public DNS and tells the caller whether the domain is free to claim on
this account.

Response shape:
    {
        "available": bool,
        "reason": null | "already_on_this_account" | "in_use_by_other_account" | "stale_records_detected",
        "detail": str | null,   # only when available=False
        "stale_tokens": int | null  # only when reason == "stale_records_detected"
    }
Get domain details including DNS records.

post.description: Trigger DNS verification for a domain.
Rotate DKIM keys for a domain. Generates a new key pair and returns the new DNS record value.

get.description:
Return what's currently published at each tracked record's host.

The domain detail page uses this to show users a real-time diff: "you
expected X, we found Y" - which is the fastest way to spot a typo, an
extra trailing dot, or a stray record from a previous attempt. Lookups
run in parallel across the small fixed set of records (typically 5), so
the total wall time is just one DNS round-trip + thread overhead.
Detect DNS issues and return actionable diagnostics.
Check MX record status for email forwarding.

Consolidated DNS health: one verdict + plain-language recommendation
per check (DKIM, SPF, DMARC, bounce routing, MX). The customer owns
their DNS, so this surfaces exactly what they need to fix and how.
Quick check if a domain is registered and verified. Lookup by name.
Detect the DNS provider for a domain via NS record lookup.

Return a signed Domain Connect apply URL.

The frontend redirects the user to this URL. The registrar shows a
summary of the DNS records that will be added and an Authorize button.

Whether one-click setup will work for this domain's DNS provider.

Returns {"supported": bool, "provider": str | null}. The frontend uses this
to show the one-click button (supported) or lead with manual records and
name the provider (unsupported).

post.description:
Begin a source-initiated transfer of this domain to another account.

Runs the hard preconditions (no active transfer, no in-flight broadcasts
or scheduled emails, target must be an Axene user), then emails the target
a 7-day accept link. Ownership does not move until the target accepts and
the 24-hour cool-off elapses.
Send an email via the API. Pass send_at for scheduled delivery (Starter+ only).

get.description:
List emails for the active workspace with optional status filter and
pagination. Always scoped via scope_filter to the active org, for admins
too. Cross-workspace admin viewing lives at /v1/admin/emails.

schema.title: Response List Emails Endpoint V1 Emails  Get

post.description: Send batch emails. Limit depends on plan (25/50/100). Starter+ only.

get.description: List all scheduled (not yet sent) emails with countdown.

delete.description: Cancel a scheduled email before it sends.

post.description: Send a scheduled email immediately instead of waiting.

Dry-run validation - checks if an email WOULD send without actually sending.
Validates: sender address, domain verification, plan limits, account restrictions.

get.description:
Search emails by recipient, sender, subject, status, or tags.

The free-text `q` parameter accepts inline tokens that translate to
structured filters:
  to:gmail.com        : substring match on recipient list
  from:hello@axene.io : substring match on from_address
  status:bounced      : exact status (overrides ?status= if both set)
  domain:axene.io     : substring match on from_address (sending domain)
  tag:welcome         : exact tag match
Any remaining whitespace-separated tokens fall back to free-text ILIKE
across from / subject / to.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Free-text + token syntax (to:, from:, status:, domain:, tag:)
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Free-text + token syntax (to:, from:, status:, domain:, tag:)

get.description: Get emails updated after the given timestamp (for polling live status changes).

parameters.name: since

schema.description: ISO 8601 timestamp to fetch updates after
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp to fetch updates after

schema.title: Response Get Email Updates V1 Emails Updates Get

get.description: The current user's saved email-log searches.
Get detailed email information including events.

post.description:
Retry a previously failed/bounced/rejected email. Creates a new Email row
linked back to the original via retry_of_id. The original row is preserved.

get.description: Get all events for a specific email.

schema.title: Response Get Email Events Endpoint V1 Emails  Email Id  Events Get
Response List Api Keys V1 Api Keys  Get
Response List Webhooks V1 Webhooks  Get

post.description:
Fire a sample `email.delivered` event at the endpoint so the caller
can verify their integration. Delivery is logged like any other and
is visible under the webhook's deliveries.

get.description: List delivery attempts for a webhook the caller owns.
Full request / response for a single delivery attempt.
List all contact lists for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Contact Lists V1 Contacts  Get

post.description: Create a new contact list.

get.description: Get a contact list with paginated contacts.

patch.description: Update a contact list's name, description, or icon.

delete.description: Delete a contact list and all its contacts.

post.description: Add a single contact to a list.

delete.description: Remove a contact from a list.

post.description:
Upload a CSV file to import contacts into a list.

The CSV must have a header row. The email column is auto-detected (looks for
columns named 'email', 'Email', 'e-mail', 'EMAIL', or the first column
containing @ signs). All other columns are stored as metadata on each contact.
Duplicate emails within the same list are skipped.

Send an email to all contacts in a list.

Template variables are supported in subject, html, and text fields:
- {{email}} - the contact's email address
- {{name}} - the contact's name
- {{any_metadata_key}} - any key from the contact's metadata

get.description: List all email forwards for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Forwards V1 Forwards  Get

post.description: Create a new email forward.

patch.description: Update an email forward's destination or active status.

get.description: List forwarded email logs for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Forwarded Emails V1 Forwards Logs Get

get.description: Get total count of forwarded emails for pagination.
Get forwarding stats for the last 30 days.
List broadcasts visible to the caller (scoped to active org).

schema.title: Response List Broadcasts V1 Broadcasts  Get

get.description:
Most-clicked links in a broadcast.

Aggregates `click` EmailEvents for every Email belonging to the
broadcast, grouped by the destination URL stored in the event
metadata. Returns the top N by click count.

post.description:
Hand the broadcast off to a background Celery worker. We validate
contact-list + sender-address visibility up front, mark the row as
`sending`, and the task does the heavy enumeration so the HTTP
request stays fast even for 50k+ recipient lists.

Each recipient ends up as a regular `Email` row with `broadcast_id`
set, which feeds the event-driven counter rollup (delivered/bounced
/opened/clicked) on the broadcast itself.

Send a winning variant to the audience that did NOT receive the test
sample. Only valid on a sampled A/B broadcast (ab_test_pct set) whose
test send has completed and that hasn't already been promoted.

schema.title: Response List Variants V1 Broadcasts  Broadcast Id  Variants Get

post.description: Duplicate a template.

get.description: List suppressed email addresses.

post.description: Add an email to the suppression list.

delete.description: Remove an email from the suppression list.

post.description: Upload CSV of emails to suppress. One email per line. Starter+ only.

get.description: Check BIMI readiness for a domain.

post.description: Upload an SVG logo for BIMI.
Enable BIMI for a domain (requires DMARC enforcement + logo).

EmailAttachment.title: EmailAttachment
EmailAttachment.description:
Single file attachment.

`content_base64` is the file body as base64 (no `data:` URL prefix).
We cap individual files at 24 MiB to keep total message size under
Postfix's 25 MiB `message_size_limit` once headers/encoding overhead
is added. The aggregate cap is enforced separately in the API layer.

ProfileRequest.title: ProfileRequest
ProfileRequest.description:
Richer onboarding persona. Every field is optional so the wizard can
save incrementally as the user moves through the profile step.

TransferEligibility.title: TransferEligibility
TransferEligibility.description:
Whether the authenticated user can accept an incoming transfer right
now. Lets the UI show a blocker (e.g. plan domain limit) before Accept.
